content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 13, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 13, 2026, focused on finalizing the Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2) infrastructure. Key achievements include arming the apps daemon, resolving critical database leaks, and stabilizing the staging environment. The team successfully transitioned to a modular IaC-driven deployment, ensuring isolated tenant database management and robust automated workflows for app lifecycle operations.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Apps Infrastructure & Daemon Arming**
*   Enabled `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` on staging to trigger app deployments now that the data plane is live ([#8406](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8406)).
*   Implemented an automated, idempotent workflow to arm the apps daemon, eliminating the need for manual SSH intervention ([#8398](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8398), [#8397](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8397)).
*   Configured the daemon to fetch tenant DSNs from shared terraform outputs, enhancing security by masking sensitive credentials ([#8404](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8404)).
*   Resolved permission issues during daemon arming by routing environment file operations through sudo ([#8405](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8405)).

**Database Management & Resource Cleanup**
*   Fixed a critical resource leak where deleted apps with isolated databases failed to drop Postgres instances or release cluster slots ([#8401](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8401)).
*   Corrected terraform lockfile issues by adding missing `linux_amd64` provider hashes, enabling successful CI planning and application ([#8402](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8402)).
*   Introduced a one-shot workflow to remove orphaned state entries, preventing provider dependency errors during infrastructure updates ([#8400](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8400)).

**Testing & Runtime Stability**
*   Added 50 characterization tests for the agent warm-pool decision engine to ensure reliable container readiness ([#8403](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8403)).
*   Refined local runtime state management to reset stale `cloud-inference` data upon selection and reduced debug log spam ([#8263](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8263)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8408](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8408): CI optimization to gate expensive test lanes based on changed paths.
*   [#8407](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8407): Feature to arm build-from-repo functionality on the daemon.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   *None.*

## NEW ISSUES
*   *None.*

## ACTIVE ISSUES

**Steward Login & Auth Hardening ([#8257](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8257))**
*   **Status:** Resolved.
*   **Summary:** Following the implementation of PKCE and signing proxy updates, production verification confirms that both Google OAuth and email magic-link logins are functioning correctly. The 400 errors previously blocking access have been eliminated. The team recommends closing the issue pending a final end-to-end sign-in confirmation.

**Eliza Cloud Apps Scaling & Ingress ([#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321))**
*   **Status:** Near completion.
*   **Summary:** The infrastructure is fully built and verified on staging. The team has successfully adopted tenant databases into the `apps-shared` module and armed the provisioning worker. The remaining blockers are minor administrative actions: flipping the `join_mode` for the staging tenant to 'open' and finalizing the build-from-repo registry configuration. Once these are addressed, the team plans to deploy a test application to prove the end-to-end chain.
